content.claim: Hungary has the highest level of segregation of students from advantaged and disadvantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools in the EU, as measured in the 2022 PISA study and documented in the 2025 EU Education and Training Monitor.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Segregation is multifactorial (residential patterns, school choice, admissions autonomy); the independent causal contribution of the 6/8-year track is not empirically isolated
Cross-sectional data does not establish causation
Other European systems with similar structural features (Austria, Germany) show lower segregation, suggesting institutional implementation rather than structure alone drives outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
